_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to organise data and create representations — like tables, graphs and models — that reveal patterns and relationships.

_source.w: We are learning to organise data and create representations — like tables, graphs and models — that reveal patterns and relationships.
y: Well-organised data helps us see connections and patterns that aren't obvious in raw numbers.
Choosing the right type of graph or model makes information easier for others to understand.
Data literacy — being able to read and create data displays — is an essential skill in today's world.
eg: Creating a line graph to show how temperature changed over the course of a day.
Using a scatter plot to show the relationship between plant height and amount of sunlight.
Building a 3D model of the layers of the Earth using clay.
Constructing a table with clear headings, units and averages from an experiment.
Drawing a labelled diagram to explain how a circuit works.
Using a pie chart to display the proportions of different materials in a soil sample.
